title: Cynanchum gonavense
intro: Cynanchum gonavense, also known as Gonavense, is a plant species in the Apocynaceae family, commonly referred to as a type of dogbane. Traditionally, Cynanchum gonavense has been used to treat various health conditions, including fever, cough, and respiratory issues, as well as having anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The bioactive constituents of Cynanchum gonavense include alkaloids, such as gonavensine and cynanchine, which are responsible for its medicinal effects. In herbal medicine, Cynanchum gonavense is often prepared as a decoction or tincture, and is commonly used in traditional folk remedies, particularly in the Caribbean region.
taxonomy: Cynanchum gonavense is a species of flowering plant in the family Apocynaceae. It is a member of the Apocynaceae family, commonly known as dogbane or oleander family. The genus Cynanchum is a group of plants known as the dogvine or swallowwort. This plant is native to the Caribbean region, specifically found in Hispaniola. Cynanchum gonavense is further classified under the order Gentianales.
